hypothesis: If catalytic surface compositions are generated by a conditional latent diffusion model operating in a chemically informed autoencoder’s shell-wise periodic-table latent space and conditioned on adsorbate identity, target adsorption energy, and allowed elements, then the resulting designs will achieve lower Fréchet distance, lower Leakage, and higher Cosine similarity than a chemically agnostic baseline even with reduced training data, because the representation encodes periodic chemical trends and local environment information that improves constraint compliance and data efficiency.
